entries.title: Monaco GP
entries.headline: Can Anyone Stop Antonelli? Mercedes' Monaco Weakness Could Change Everything
entries.summary: Kimi Antonelli rolls into Monaco on a four-race winning streak, but this is the one track where Mercedes have never looked comfortable — and Ferrari are ready to pounce.
entries.body:
Kimi Antonelli has been absolutely on fire lately, winning four races in a row to build a 43-point lead over his own teammate George Russell. Canada was particularly spicy — the two Mercedes drivers were racing each other hard in both the Sprint and the Grand Prix, with Russell taking the sprint and Antonelli coming out on top in the feature race. Russell even admitted after the race that the championship might already be Antonelli's to lose. As a team Mercedes are miles ahead in the constructors' standings, but Monaco is a different animal entirely, and history hasn't been kind to them here.

Ferrari haven't won a race yet this season but this weekend could be their moment. Their car loves slow corners, and Monaco is basically nothing but slow corners. Charles Leclerc is also from Monaco, races here like he knows every inch of the barriers — because he does — and has been agonisingly close to winning his home race before. On top of that the FIA has tweaked the rules specifically for Monaco, removing the straight mode that usually gives Mercedes an edge on the few short straights the circuit has. Qualifying on Saturday is everything here — overtaking is nearly impossible in the race — so if Leclerc can nail a front-row start, don't be surprised if Ferrari finally get that first win of 2026 on Sunday.
